result:
Build is green (warnings only). No temporary instrumentation was added (none was needed — the bug was visible from static reading).

## Report

**Root cause:** `debug.upvalueid` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/debug_lib.rs:589-607` was a Phase-B placeholder: `check_upval` correctly looked up the upvalue identity, but the `Some(uid)` arm threw the identity away and called `state.push_fail()` (push nil), identical to the `None` arm. So every call returned `nil`, breaking the assertions in `goto.lua:194` (`assert(debug.upvalueid(a[1], 2) ~= debug.upvalueid(a[i], 2))`) — `nil ~= nil` is false.

**Fix:** In the `Some(uid)` arm, push the identity as a light userdata: `lua_vm::api::push_light_userdata(state, uid as *mut core::ffi::c_void)`. `LightUserData` is the documented allowed raw-pointer case (PORTING.md §3.1) and matches C-Lua's `lua_pushlightuserdata(L, id)`. `GcRef::identity` already provides stable per-upvalue identity values that compare correctly with `==`/`~=`.

**New state:** The original failure at combined-line 202 (upvalueid identity test in the `foo()` closures) is resolved. The test now runs much further and fails at combined-line 275 (`::L3:: assert(X == true)`) — the `<close>` to-be-closed-variable metamethod test (test line 266). That is a separate bug (missing `__close` invocation on scope exit), outside the scope of this fix.

tool_input.command: cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; echo "exit: ${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
